description: Static, machine-readable manifest of the authorized PRJCT9 Daily Signal Board operator commands as of Phase 6H-6. Describes commands; does not execute them. The accompanying runbook at md_library/shared/2026-05-12_PHASE_6H7_PRODUCTION_RUNBOOK.md is the human-readable companion.

required_env_var.name: PRJCT9_AUTOMATION_WRITE_AUTH
required_env_var.purpose: Second key of the Phase 6H-5 two-key write authorization. Required IN ADDITION to the CLI --write flag before any live refresher / pipeline write fires.

read_only_commands.label: cache_cutoff_watcher
read_only_commands.purpose: Read cache.last_date and compare to current_as_of_date. Reports recommended_operator_action.

read_only_commands.label: automation_preflight
read_only_commands.purpose: Per-ticker orchestration plan with StackBuilder inventory + selected variant + advisory would_run_commands.

read_only_commands.label: source_freshness_preflight
read_only_commands.purpose: Refresh-focused per-ticker classification (refresh_source_cache / run_pipeline_after_refresh / pipeline_output_lags_persist_skip / etc.).

read_only_commands.label: launch_readiness_audit
read_only_commands.purpose: Full per-stage readiness verdict with optional pipeline-runner dry-run.

read_only_commands.label: refresher_dry_run
read_only_commands.purpose: Heaviest read-only probe. Touches yfinance to peek at the next available trading-day close without writing.

dry_run_commands.label: automation_executor_dry_run
dry_run_commands.purpose: Dry-run executor with refresh -> recheck -> pipeline sequencing. Pipeline write command intentionally withheld until watcher recheck passes. --write is rejected with rc=2.

dry_run_commands.label: automation_writer_dry_run
dry_run_commands.purpose: Guarded write-capable executor running in default dry-run mode. No --write, no env var. No writes.

dry_run_commands.label: automation_writer_explicit_dry_run
dry_run_commands.purpose: Same as above with the explicit --dry-run flag. Identical behavior; flag exists for operator clarity.

authorized_writer_command.label: authorized_writer
authorized_writer_command.purpose: The one path to authorized live writes. Refresh -> watcher recheck -> pipeline (only if watcher returns ready_for_pipeline_write). Writes a status JSON to <STATUS_DIR>, a cache PKL + manifest sidecar to <CACHE_DIR>, and a Phase 6D Confluence artifact to <ARTIFACT_ROOT>. Appends one row to <EXECUTION_LOG_PATH>.

exit_codes.0: execution completed; report emitted to stdout
exit_codes.2: invalid CLI arguments OR --write requested without the matching env-var value

prohibited_commands.label: universe_sweep
prohibited_commands.reason: Daily automation runs against an explicit operator-named ticker list. There is no production scheduler today; a universe sweep would silently amplify any error across the entire saved catalogue. Never invoke any writer with a wildcard or --all-tickers / --universe flag.

prohibited_commands.label: stackbuilder_execution
prohibited_commands.reason: StackBuilder is a manual research workflow. Saved stack variants are durable inputs to the daily automation, not daily-rebuilt outputs. The Phase 6H automation chain explicitly forbids invoking StackBuilder.

prohibited_commands.label: onepass_execution
prohibited_commands.reason: OnePass is a research workflow that produces signal-library inputs. It is a stable manual input to StackBuilder, not a daily automation step.

prohibited_commands.label: multitimeframe_library_rebuild
prohibited_commands.reason: Multi-timeframe libraries are stable / manual inputs. The daily automation blocks with refresh_multitimeframe_libraries_manual rather than rebuilding them itself.

prohibited_commands.label: writer_with_only_cli_flag_no_env
prohibited_commands.reason: The two-key authorization requires both --write AND the env var. --write alone returns rc=2 and writes nothing; that is by design. Do not work around it.

prohibited_commands.label: writer_against_production_without_status_dir_override
prohibited_commands.reason: Authorized writes MUST supply --status-dir explicitly. Omitting it (or pointing it at the production project/cache/status/ during a temp-dir rehearsal) leaks the per-ticker status JSON outside the operator-controlled root. The Phase 6H-6 plumbing was added specifically to close this gap.

decision_tree.use_command: authorized_writer (refresher runs; watcher rechecks; pipeline only if watcher returns ready_for_pipeline_write)
authorized_writer (pipeline runs directly; no refresh needed)

safety_contract_summary.two_key_write_authorization: Both --write AND PRJCT9_AUTOMATION_WRITE_AUTH=phase_6h5_explicit are required. Either missing -> rc=2, no writes.
safety_contract_summary.refresh_recheck_pipeline_sequencing: Under authorized writes for the refresh-then-pipeline branch, the refresher runs first, then cache_cutoff_watcher rechecks against the post-refresh cache, then the pipeline runner runs ONLY when the watcher returns ready_for_pipeline_write. Any other watcher verdict (including pipeline_output_lags_persist_skip) withholds the pipeline write.
safety_contract_summary.persist_skip_lag_rule: After a refresh, the cache_date_range_end MUST be strictly greater than current_as_of_date for the watcher to return ready_for_pipeline_write. If they are equal, Phase 6D-1's persist_skip_bars=1 trim would land Confluence one trading bar behind the cutoff and the pipeline write is correctly withheld.
safety_contract_summary.stackbuilder_policy: Saved stack variants are durable inputs. The automation preserves the existing pipeline default (newest-mtime under stackbuilder/<TICKER>/) and surfaces the selected run via stackbuilder_selection_policy=latest_mtime_existing_pipeline_default. A single saved variant is OK. Multi-variant with a clear newest mtime is OK and the latest-mtime default applies. Tied newest-mtime is ambiguous_tied_mtime and blocks automation; the operator must pick. No 30-day stale window; stack age alone is never a block.
safety_contract_summary.watcher_exception_handling: If the post-refresh watcher itself raises, the executor returns a structured TickerWriteExecution with refresh_result preserved, pipeline_result=None, issue_codes=('watcher_exception',), final_recommended_action=refresh_executed_pipeline_withheld, and one row appended to the execution log. The refresh side effect is captured; the pipeline write is withheld.
safety_contract_summary.execution_log_contract: When --execution-log <path> is supplied, the writer appends one JSON object per ticker per invocation with logged_at UTC timestamp + full per-execution payload. Append-only; never truncates.

rollback_recovery.git_does_not_track_production_cache_or_o: Production cache/, output/research_artifacts/, output/stackbuilder/, signal_library/data/stable/ are gitignored. git checkout, git reset, and git revert do NOT recover from a bad write. Rollback requires restoring files from operator-managed backups or from previous artifact copies retained outside the repo.
rollback_recovery.before_any_authorized_run: Snapshot the four operator output roots that the authorized writer can modify: <CACHE_DIR>, <STATUS_DIR>, <ARTIFACT_ROOT>, plus the <EXECUTION_LOG_PATH> file. A simple file copy or tarball is sufficient. Keep the snapshot until the run's verdict has been audited.
rollback_recovery.after_any_authorized_run: Read the execution log JSONL row for the ticker. Confirm refresh_result.succeeded=True, post_refresh_watcher_action=ready_for_pipeline_write (or absent for run_pipeline_only), and pipeline_result.leader_eligible=True. Compare the new artifact's last_date against the expected cutoff. If anything looks wrong, restore the snapshot before any further write attempts.
rollback_recovery.do_not_attempt_universe_rollback: Even a small wildcard rerun can rewrite dozens of artifacts. There is no batch rollback. Operate one ticker at a time.

do_not_run_warnings: Never invoke any automation command with --all-tickers, --universe, or any wildcard pattern.

Never invoke daily_board_automation_writer.py with --write against production paths without the corresponding env var. The CLI will refuse with rc=2, but treat the prompt itself as suspect if you find yourself trying.

Never invoke daily_board_automation_writer.py --write without --status-dir, --cache-dir, --artifact-root, --execution-log all explicitly supplied. Default paths point at production; the Phase 6H-6 plumbing exists precisely so they can be operator-named.
Never run StackBuilder or OnePass as part of the daily automation. They are stable manual research inputs. Re-running them silently rewrites operator-pinned research stacks.
Never rebuild multi-timeframe libraries from the automation chain. The planner reports refresh_multitimeframe_libraries_manual when they are missing; the operator handles that out-of-band.
Never schedule automated daily writes without first wiring the Phase 5C validation_contract_v1 sidecar integration. Phase 6H-6 doc § 8 names this as a remaining blocker.
